commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

packages alongside gcc and libpq-dev in the backend Docker image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

merge: integrate main (CAE sub-tabs + Railway overhaul) into sanarQubIssuesFIxes

Conflicts resolved:
- backend/Dockerfile: combined non-root user security fix with start.sh entrypoint,
  merged consecutive RUN instructions into one layer
- frontend/components/modules/cae-tab.tsx: took main's rewrite (sub-tabs + useToast),
  our alert() fix was already absorbed
- PROGRESS.md: manual merge, appended Etape 14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties
  to avoid false positives on JSX component imports that SonarCloud misidentifies as type-only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): rebuild HEMesh after push-pull so face splitting works

After push-pull creates/modifies geometry via Manifold, the HEMesh
becomes stale. Now we rebuild it from the new BufferGeometry so that
line-tool face splitting works on push-pull-created boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): build HEMesh directly at box creation, avoid lossy conversion

The round-trip BoxGeometry → HEMesh → BufferGeometry was corrupting
geometry. Now boxes are created with HEMesh as the source of truth,
and LineTool only attempts face splitting on objects that already
have an HEMesh (no more ensureHEMesh conversion).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): use point-in-face lookup instead of stale triangle index

ensureHEMesh() rebuilds the geometry from the HEMesh, which changes all
triangle indices. The targetFaceIndex captured during pickDrawingPlane
was stale by the time finalizeLine used it.

Fix: add findFaceAtPoint() which does a 3D point-on-plane check + 2D
point-in-polygon test to find the correct HE face from the line's start
point, independent of triangle indices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

Site-rate heterogeneity: document stackless variant of Vose's alias method

algorithm hinted at in Vose 1991, Section VI (two scanning pointers + a
temp variable instead of explicit small/large stacks).  Add code comment
in alias_sampler.h pointing to the plan and explaining why we prefer the
explicit stacks.

12 skills (6,900+ lines):
- agent-packaging-foundations, mcp-server-packaging, claude-code-plugin-packaging
- custom-gpt-packaging, docker-agent-packaging, pypi-agent-packaging

10 commands across 3 phases:
- ASSESS: /agent-guide, /agent-audit

- SHIP: /gen-install, /pre-ship, /secure
- Standalone: /agent-roast, /glossary

17 frameworks: LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Pydantic AI, LlamaIndex,
Semantic Kernel, Vercel AI SDK, OpenAI Assistants, Anthropic Tool Use,
Smolagents, n8n, Flowise, Dify, ComfyUI, Playwright, Puppeteer, custom.

10 distribution targets: MCP Server (npm/PyPI), Claude Code Plugin,
Custom GPT, Docker, PyPI, npm, HuggingFace, Replicate, Cloud Deploy.

Academic foundations: Cognitive Load Theory (Sweller 1988), Fogg Behavior
Model, Hick's Law, Nielsen heuristics, Twelve-Factor App, OWASP, SLSA.
115 references in REFERENCES.md. All skills cite official documentation.

11,211 total lines across 28 files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add half-edge mesh kernel with face splitting

Implements a custom half-edge data structure (DCEL) as the topological
foundation for SketchUp-like 3D modeling operations.

Phase 1 - Kernel Core:
- HEMesh class with traversal (faceLoop, faceVertices, adjacentFaces) and
  mutation (addFace, splitFace, splitEdge, removeFace)
- Ear-clipping triangulation for concave polygon faces
- BufferGeometry ↔ HEMesh conversion with vertex welding and coplanar
  triangle merging
- JSON serialization/deserialization

Phase 2 - Integration Bridge:
- ensureHEMesh() lazily upgrades CAD objects to use topology
- syncMeshFromHE() regenerates Three.js geometry after mutations
- buildFaceDataFromHE() provides O(1) face lookup vs O(n) coplanar scan
- heMesh persisted alongside geometry in save/load

Phase 3 - Face Splitting:
- findLineFaceIntersection() detects where lines cross face boundaries
- splitFaceByLineSegment() splits faces into independent polygons
- LineTool attempts topological face split before falling back to thin box

54 unit tests passing (39 kernel + 15 face split).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GitHub Actions auto-deploy workflow

Deploys to Hostinger via SCP on push to main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Switch to sql.js (pure JS/WASM) for universal Node.js compatibility

Native SQLite bindings (sqlite3, better-sqlite3) fail on platforms
without prebuilt binaries for the target Node version. sql.js uses
WASM and works on any Node version without compilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(navigate): region-based terrain — WoW-style painted biome zones, connected features

ARCHITECTURE CHANGE: Per-hex rendering → region-based rendering
- _buildBiomeRegions(): groups adjacent same-biome hexes into connected clusters
- _hullPath(): convex hull with organic wobble for painting region boundaries
- _hexVisibility(): centralized visibility calculation with extended range (d<=6)

CONNECTED TERRAIN (mountains overlap, forests merge, no empty space):
- _drawConnectedMountains(): continuous ridgeline across cluster, 3-6 peaks per hex,
  10-14 line hatching per peak, cross-hatching, snow caps, scree, base shadows
- _drawConnectedForest(): merged canopy spanning multiple hexes, "bridge" blobs
  between adjacent hexes fill gaps, unified shadow mass, 8-14 blobs per hex
- _drawConnectedSnow(): icy peaks with glacier bands, snow drifts
- All other biomes (swamp, desert, volcanic, cave, graveyard, grassland) upgraded

PAINTED BIOME ZONES:
- Region tints now use hull-based shapes at 0.55 * visibility opacity (was 0.28)
- Inner gradient blobs for depth on large clusters
- Biome colors strengthened across the board
- Visibility boosted: d<=1 now 0.85/1.0, d<=2 0.65/0.8 (was 0.65/0.85)
- No auto-fill opacity penalty — all terrain renders at near-full visibility

Fix event mode bugs: daypart slots, genreHint filtering, regeneration instructions

- Use eventTimeRange for daypart generation in events (was using venue hours,
  causing fallback to generic Morning/Afternoon/Evening on regeneration)
- Add penalty for playlists matching zero genreHints when 3+ hints given
  (prevents unrelated cuisine playlists like Indian/Chinese scoring via category)
- System prompt: pass event time range as both hours and eventTimeRange,
  don't re-lookup on regeneration, strict genreHints for event themes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dataset definition and instantiation engine (20 MCP tools, 90 total)

Implement a business-aggregation-driven dataset engine that allows defining
reusable dataset templates with node trees, relation rules, field mappings
and transform rules. Supports versioned lifecycle (DRAFT→PUBLISHED→ARCHIVED),
async execution with topological ordering, nested JSON assembly, and result
snapshot storage with SHA-256 hashing.

- 8 new entities, 2 enums, 8 repositories, 10 DTOs
- 3 services: DatasetDefinitionService (CRUD + Kahn cycle detection),
  DatasetExecutionService (async engine), DatasetTransformEngine (transforms)
- REST API under /api/datasets, 20 MCP tools in DatasetTools
- SQL injection protection, per-node maxRows, query timeout, concurrent exec guard

commit.message: infra: 온프렘 CI/CD 파이프라인 구성 (#31) * infra: 온프렘 CI/CD 파이프라인 구성 - 로컬 CI 스크립트 (scripts/ci-local.sh): full/quick/test/build/docker 모드 - Git pre-push hook (.githooks/pre-push): push 전 lint+typecheck 자동 실행 - GitHub Actions self-hosted runner 설치 스크립트 (scripts/setup-runner.sh) - CI workflow를 self-hosted runner 기본 사용으로 변경 + bun 캐시 추가 - Makefile에 ci/ci-quick/ci-test/ci-build/ci-docker/setup-runner/setup-hooks 타겟 추가 - CLAUDE.md에 온프렘 CI/CD 문서 반영 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: 품질 개선 + 운영 준비 - preview 컴포넌트 → next/image 변환 (ESLint 0 warnings) - TypeScript 진단 오류 수정 (0 errors) - .env.production.example 생성 (프로덕션 환경 변수 템플릿) - docker-compose.prod.yml 생성 (restart policy, healthcheck, 리소스 제한) - /api/health 엔드포인트 추가 - Makefile prod 타겟 추가 (prod-up, prod-down, prod-build, prod-logs) - CLAUDE.md 프로덕션 배포 문서 반영 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: CI self-hosted runner에 unzip 의존성 설치 스텝 추가 oven-sh/setup-bun 액션이 unzip을 필요로 하나 self-hosted runner에 미설치되어 CI 실패. 각 job에 조건부 unzip 설치 스텝 추가. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * revert: CI에서 unzip 설치 스텝 제거 (runner에 직접 설치) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse CI 실행 전 포트 3000 정리 스텝 추가 self-hosted runner에서 이전 프로세스가 포트 3000을 점유하여 Lighthouse 실패. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse CI 포트를 9456으로 변경하여 충돌 방지 self-hosted runner에서 포트 3000이 점유되어 Lighthouse 실패. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse 포트 3000 복원 (충돌 원인 Docker 컨테이너 제거됨) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse CI 포트 9456 사용 (self-hosted runner 포트 충돌 방지) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse CI standalone 서버 사용 + 포트 9456 output: standalone 설정에 맞게 node .next/standalone/server.js 사용. 포트 9456으로 self-hosted runner 충돌 방지. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse startServerCommand에서 node → bun 사용 self-hosted runner에 node가 PATH에 없어서 bun으로 standalone 서버 실행. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse job에 setup-node 추가 (Lighthouse CLI가 node 필요) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 * fix: Lighthouse CI를 ubuntu-latest에서 실행 + standalone 설정 정비 - Lighthouse job만 ubuntu-latest 사용 (Chrome, Node.js 기본 포함) - standalone output에 맞게 node .next/standalone/server.js 실행 - 빌드 후 public/, .next/static/ 파일 standalone 디렉토리에 복사 - 포트 3000 복원 (깨끗한 환경이므로 충돌 없음) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
rename crew/section vocabulary to stage throughout codebase

Unifies the pipeline stage terminology — "crew" and "section" were
confusing because "crew" sounds like a team of people, not a pipeline
stage. Now consistently called "stage" everywhere: types, schemas,
components, MCP tools, templates, agent definitions, and docs.

Key renames:
- CrewConfig → StageConfig, crewId → stageId, crews → stages
- 9 component/utility files renamed (CrewHeader→StageHeader, etc.)
- MCP tools: list_crews → list_stages, create_crew → create_stage, etc.
- department.yaml: crews: → stages:
- All 12 agent .md files updated
- 122 files changed total

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix payment return: auto-switch to billing tab when payment_id in URL

The BillingPage component handles payment_id polling, but it only renders
when the billing tab is active. On return from Mollie, the default tab was
'upload', so the payment status poll never ran. Now App detects payment_id
in URL params at init and sets the initial tab to 'billing'.

Also fixes TS build errors (unused params in api.ts).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename to SwiftInjectable, @Injectable macro, module split example

- Rename SwiftInjectableMacros → SwiftInjectable module
- @Dependencies → @Injectable macro (reads lazy var, generates registerAll + init)
- DependenciesStore → InjectionStore with type-keyed registration
- Add InjectableContainer protocol + .injectAll() for bulk injection
- Split Example into Domain/ConsoleLogger/LiveAPIClient/Presentation/App modules
- Hooks now @Inject individual protocols instead of whole container

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

style: improve dork builder label readability

- Field labels now use var(--text-bright) via .dork-builder class
- Platform category labels use .platform-cat class with var(--text-mid) at 10px
- Fix // DORK BUILDER heading to var(--text-bright)
- Remove inline color overrides from all label tags

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): throw clear error when media_type missing in Gemini inlineData parts

Previously buildGeminiImageParts/AudioParts/DocumentParts would silently pass
an empty mimeType to the Gemini API, producing a cryptic "Unsupported MIME type:"
error with no actionable message. Now all three functions throw immediately with
the block index and expected field name (media_type) when the value is falsy.

Bumps @crystralai/core to 0.4.2.

Also adds examples/ad-template-generator — a full-stack NestJS + Next.js demo
of Crystal AI's GoogleProvider, withRetry, Logger, and hashCacheKey.
